name: iminuit (Python Interface to MINUIT2 Optimizer)

performance.notes: MINUIT2 C++ backend; extremely fast for likelihood minimization

agent_readiness.notes: Python interface to MINUIT2 for likelihood/chi-squared minimization. from iminuit import Minuit. def fcn(mu, sigma): return chi2 or -2*log_likelihood. m = Minuit(fcn, mu=0.0, sigma=1.0). m.errordef = Minuit.LIKELIHOOD. m.migrad(). m.hesse(). m.minos(). m.values['mu']. m.errors['mu']. m.covariance. m.valid. m.draw_mncontour('mu', 'sigma'). Gradient support. Least squares: from iminuit.cost import UnbinnedNLL, BinnedNLL, LeastSquares. cost = UnbinnedNLL(data, pdf). Jupyter visualization.

gotchas: LGPL-2.1 — must link dynamically or disclose modifications in commercial use
errordef must be set correctly (0.5 for log-likelihood, 1.0 for chi-squared)
MINOS asymmetric error estimation is slow for high-dimensional fits
Understanding MINUIT convergence status (MIGRAD valid/covariance accurate) required
best_when: Maximum likelihood or chi-squared fitting in HEP/particle physics
When robust error estimation (MINOS, HESSE) is required
Fast optimizer for unbinned/binned likelihood fits
avoid_when: General optimization without gradient information (use scipy.optimize)
Bayesian posterior sampling (use emcee or PyMC)
summary: iminuit provides Python access to CERN's MINUIT2 optimizer for maximum likelihood fitting. Minuit(fcn, mu=0.0, sigma=1.0).migrad().hesse().minos(). m.values, m.errors, m.covariance. MINOS asymmetric errors. draw_mncontour for 2D profiles. cost.UnbinnedNLL, BinnedNLL. LGPL-2.1 by CERN/scikit-HEP. Gold standard for HEP likelihood minimization. errordef must be set (0.5 for LL, 1.0 for chi2).
